text: Which of the following Agile Manifesto principles is the most related to team empowerment?
explanation: Since the development team is such an important factor, agile methods promote empowered teams. People work better when they are given the autonomy to organize and plan their own work. Agile methods advocate freeing the team from the micromanagement. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 8 and 33

choices.text: The most efficient and effective method of conveying information to and within a development team is face-to-face conversation.
Build projects around motivated individuals. Give them the environment and support they need, and trust them to get the job done.
Working software is the primary measure of progress.
Agile processes promote sustainable development. The sponsors, developers, and users should be able to maintain a constant pace indefinitely.

text: The organization has recently signed a contract with the government of two countries to build a very large bridge linking the two countries crossing a sea. The required work is very large and complex, it is budgeted at 2 billions $ and scheduled to be completed within 3 years. The required work can be best described as:
explanation: Programs are not large projects. A very large project may be referred to as a megaproject. As a guideline, megaprojects cost US$1billion or more, affect 1 million or more people, and run for years. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 10

text: You are a project manager for a software development project. You have completed project planning and started project executing. You discover that a stakeholder always comes to the project team office and asks the team members many questions about the project. What should you do with him?
explanation: The best method is to follow communication management plan. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 389

choices.text: Meet with the stakeholder to understand why he feels the need to communicate with your team members.
Ask the stakeholder to formally address his questions to you.
Tell your team members not to waste their time talking this stakeholder.
Ask the stakeholder to formally address project issues as per the communications management plan.

text: You are managing a construction project which contains a procurement work. The air conditioning system of the project will be outsourced to a contractor. You prepare the RFP to be sent to contractors when your cousin knows that you will need a contractor. He owns a company working in air conditioning system and he wants to bid on your project. The reputation of his company is very good and your company worked with him before and he's done good work. What should you do?
explanation: When bidders are competing for a contract, you must make sure that no bidders receive preferential treatment. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 487

choices.text: You disclose the conflict of interest to your company, and disqualify your cousin’s company.
You disclose the conflict of interest to your company, and remove yourself from the selection committee.
You disclose the conflict of interest to your company, and provide your cousin with information that the other bidders don’t have so that he has a better chance of winning the contract.
You do not disclose the conflict of interest, and give your cousin the bid.

text: A software organization is moving towards agile approach in managing its projects. This approach is new to the organization, and the project manager's role with these new teams hasn't been clearly defined. Ann is a project manager in this organization. She decides to attend her team's user story writing workshops. Why does she think these sessions are a good use of her limited time?
explanation: Attending the user story writing workshop will build good relationships with team members and will help her understand the features of the product as they will be broken down into user stories. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 58

choices.text: She wants to build strong relationships with the development team members and make sure there is a shared understanding of the product features.
She wants to build strong relationships with the business stakeholders, while the agile coach manages the development team.
She wants to make sure the requirements will deliver the planned scope and be released on time, by the project deadline.
She plans to document the requirements in a requirements traceability matrix to make sure all the work contributes to achieving the project objectives.

text: You are managing a project for establishing guidelines for handling toxic material. One of the key stakeholders required to change the duration of an activity on the critical path, which will change the schedule baseline. The change is submitted to the change control board and the change was approved. You need to update the schedule baseline to reflect the change. Where can you find the most updated version of the Schedule Baseline?
explanation: Configuration management involves making sure that everyone is working off the same documents. It describes how information about the items of the project will be recorded and updated so that the product, service or result of the project remains consistent. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 88

text: The PMO is trying to select one of the potential projects to implement within the organization. They are preparing some financial information to calculate the potential benefits of the candidate projects. They want to calculate how much money each candidate project will return. Which of the following financial metrics should they use?
explanation: Return on Investment (ROI) measures the profitability of an investment by calculating the ratio of the benefits received from the investment to the money invested in it. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 102

text: An organization has adopted an agile approach recently for a new project. The project sponsor directs their project manager, who has been assigned as a team facilitator, to add a new high-priority feature to the product. How should the project manager handle this scope change?
explanation: It is the ultimate responsibility of the product owner to manage and prioritize the product backlog. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 52.

choices.text: The project manager should add the new feature to the product backlog and make it the top priority.
On an agile project, changes are welcomed at any time, so this feature should be assigned to the team for development.
Communicate the new feature request to the product owner to be considered in the next backlog session.
Tell the sponsor to talk with the product owner about this feature, reminding them that the product owner has authority to prioritize features.

text: Which of the following statements best describes risk audits?
explanation: In risk audits an external auditor reviews risk response strategies to ensure that risk management process are taken seriously and are used to collect lessons learned for future projects. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 456

choices.text: The project manager reviews each risk on the risk register with the team.
The project sponsor audits your work and decides whether you’re doing a good job.
An external auditor reviews the risk response strategies for each risk.
An external auditor reviews the watch-listed risks.

text: After the weekly project status meeting with the project team members, you asked a project team member to send minutes of meeting to all the attendees. Meeting minutes is classified as:
explanation: Formal Communication includes reports, formal meetings (both regular and ad hoc), meeting agendas and minutes, stakeholder briefings, and presentations. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 13

text: An agile team has committed to deliver 50 story points during the running iteration. Midway through the iteration one team member becomes sick and must have rest for the remaining time of the iteration. A team member suggests that the team can work overtime to complete the committed story points in the iteration. The team leader should inform him that his suggestion contradicts with which agile principle?
explanation: Agile methods recognize the value of a sustainable pace that allows team members to maintain a work-life balance. A sustainable pace is not only better for the team; it benefits the organization as well. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 8

choices.text: At regular intervals, the team reflects on how to become more effective, then tunes and adjusts its behavior accordingly.
The best architectures, requirements, and designs emerge from self-organizing teams.
Agile processes promote sustainable development. The sponsors, developers, and users should be able to maintain a constant pace indefinitely.
Simplicity—the art of maximizing the amount of work not done—is essential.

text: Alfrid is attending a project management training session. He is watching the speaker to pick up physical gestures and facial expressions. He confirms he is listening, expresses agreement or disagreement and summarizing what he says. Which communication skill does he have?
explanation: Listening actively means staying engaged with the speaker and summarizing conversations to ensure effective information exchange. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 363 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 12 and 21

text: The twelvth principle of the Agile Manifesto states that: "At regular intervals, the team reflects on how to become more effective, then tunes and adjusts its behavior accordingly". Which of the following agile practices and techniques is closely related to this principle?
explanation: Agile methods employ frequent lookbacks, called “retrospectives,” to reflect on how things are working on the project and identify opportunities for improvements. These retrospectives are typically done at the end of each iteration. Teams can apply those improvements in the upcoming iterations. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 80

text: You are a project manager for a construction project that will install glass walls for a new company. The contract between your company and the customer is a time and material contract which stated that your company will get $11 per meter, and your company is doing all the work. The new company has 4 equal sides of 125 meter, and requires the installation of a six-meter diameter banner on one side. Glass walls should be installed at a rate of 100 meter per day. The banner installation will cost $500 and take one day to complete. The banner must be installed before work can begin on that side of the wall. After three days of work, one side is completed, another has 75 meter installed, and the banner is completely installed. So far, $2,700 of the total project budget has been used. What is the current status of the project?
explanation: EV=(125ft+75ft)11+500 = 2700, AC=2700 Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: It is over budget with a cost variance of +$125.
The budget is neutral with cost variance of $0.
It is under budget, with a cost variance of +$500 due to the completion of the banner installation.
It is over budget, with a cost variance of -$1,100

text: Albert is managing an IT project for a multi-national organization. The project involves replacing the old intra-network with a new one. The project is now near completion. The project sponsor wants to ensure smooth transition to operations and ensure good support to the customer after project delivery. What should Albert do?
explanation: Project closure/transition involves knowledge sharing and transfer from project to operation to ensure proper support. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 123 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Ensure that knowledge is properly transferred to the operations team.
Assign one of the project team members to the operations team.
Ensure that all needed information exist in the customer operations manual.
Assign one of the operations team to join the project during project closure.

text: A change request has been approved by change control board to change the scope baseline. Which of the following documents is updated first?
explanation: The change log is normally updated after the change has been approved to reflect the change status. Then, any other document, baseline or subsidiary plan may be updated. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 120 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 79

text: During product demonstration, the team presents the product increment to the stakeholders. The customer requests some new features to be added. Which of the following is the best course of action? (1) The team should analyze the impact of adding the new features. (2) Add the new features to the next iteration. (3) Submit a change request. (4) Ask the customer about the priority of the new features. (5) The product owner should maintain the product backlog.
explanation: As the team completes the features usually in the form of user stories, the team periodically demonstrates the working product. The product owner sees the demonstration and accepts or declines stories. If the stakeholders want to add new features, they should clarify their priorities and accordingly the product owner will add them to the product backlog. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55.

text: Christian is a scrum master for a software project in his organization. He is going on a leave for 3 weeks. What should happen to the daily scrum during his absence?
explanation: In a Daily Scrum, the scrum master's role is to listen and record any obstacles to the team's progress for immediate follow up. The team is self organized and the discussion is run by the team for the team, so there is no need for facilitator. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 53

choices.text: Updates can be collected through emails.
The daily scrum should be held daily and the product owner can facilitate the event instead of the scrum master.
The daily scrum should be postponed during the absence of the scrum master.
The daily scrum should be held daily regardless the absence of the scrum master.

text: An organization adopts agile transformation. They hired an agile coach to train the teams about agile methodologies. A team member asks the agile coach: "I have the impression that agile teams do not plan upfront for their projects. Is this true?". The agile coach explains the difference between planning in predictive and adaptive environments. He can mention all of the following, except:
explanation: There is a myth saying that planning doesn't happen in agile projects. Planning is critical to project success but it is different than traditional projects. Planning is an ongoing process and provides multiple mechanisms to proactively update the plan. It is not one time stand-alone activity. Total planning may be more but distributed. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: Planning doesn't happen in agile projects as there is no
In agile projects planning is an ongoing process and provides multiple mechanisms to proactively update the plan.
In agile projects, planning is not one time stand-alone activity.
In agile projects, total planning may be more than traditional projects but it is distributed along the project.

text: A project team has created a software as a project deliverable for a customer. The customer has initiated the project and requested a software solution because they think that the solution resolves the business need for higher productivity. The software which is the output of the project does not enable the productivity outcome that is intended. The project stakeholders feel that the project has failed. Which of the following is the best course of action from the project team?
explanation: The main issue here is the focus on deliverables not on the intended outcomes. To support value realization from projects, project teams shift focus from deliverables to the intended outcomes. Doing so allows project teams to deliver on the vision or purpose of the project, rather than simply creating a specific deliverable. Domain = Business Environment. The Standard for Project Management, Page 36

choices.text: Ask customer to issue a change request and include the needed modifications to the software to be assessed by the change control board.
No action needed, the project team has delivered the project deliverable which was requested by the customer.
Adding a new deliverable of training and coaching on the use of the software to enable a better productivity outcome.
Close the current project and start a new one with clear objectives.

text: Which of the following Agile Manifesto principles is more related to Working Collaboratively?
explanation: The Agile Manifesto highlights stakeholder collaboration in its fourth principle “Business people and developers must work together daily throughout the project”. By working with business representatives daily, we can learn about the business in a way that is far beyond what a collection of requirements-gathering meetings can ever achieve. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 8 and 35

choices.text: Business people and developers must work together daily throughout the project.
Welcome changing requirements, even late in development. Agile processes harness change for the customer’s competitive advantage.
Simplicity—the art of maximizing the amount of work not done—is essential.
Deliver working software frequently, from a couple of weeks to a couple of months, with a preference to the shorter timescale.

text: Peter is the project manager for a large project to build a bridge that links two cities crossing a river. The project has a high risk score. He is working with his team to plan the risk responses for the project to decrease the project risks. He feels that the team successfully completed the risk response planning. Which of the following risk processes is repeated after the plan risk responses to determine if the overall project risk has been satisfactorily decreased?
explanation: Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis should be repeated as needed, to determine if the overall project risk has been satisfactorily decreased. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 429

text: A new project has started in an organization using adaptive approach. Few weeks later, It has been noticed that team performance has decreased because team energy is put into unproductive activities like unnecessary conflicts, many disagreements, clashes and harsh dialogues between team members. Which of the following is the best leadership style for the team coach at this stage?
explanation: As the team passes into the storming phase, there will generally be plenty of disagreement, open conflict, and harsh dialogue. During this stage, the leader needs to assume the coaching style to help team members resolve conflicts without damaging relationships. So the coaching leadership style should provide high levels of both directive and supportive leadership. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition.

text: Which of the following charts can be used to represent risks by their impact, probability and proximity?
explanation: Where risks have been categorized using more than two parameters, the probability and impact matrix cannot be used and other graphical representations are required. For example, a bubble chart displays three dimensions of data, where each risk is plotted as a disk (bubble), and the three parameters are represented by the x-axis value, the y-axis value, and the bubble size. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 425

text: Andrew is a project manager for a construction project. He is identifying risks for a new construction project with his team members and stakeholders. They discovered that the wall painting material they are using on the building cannot be used in rainy conditions. They will use these paints during the time when most of the rainfall happens in this part of the country. The project can’t be delayed after the rainy season and he needs to make sure the building is painted. He decides that his team will just have to allow enough time in the schedule for non-worked rain days. This is an example of which risk response strategy?
explanation: The team can’t do anything about the weather, so the project manager has accepted the fact that they could end up being delayed by it. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 443 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123

text: Samuel is managing a project to develop a new pharmaceutical compound. He noticed that the packing process has many problems, so he held a meeting with his team to examine problems and non-value-added activities that occur during packing process. They also identified many opportunities to improve the process. Which process are they involved in and which tool are they using?
explanation: Process analysis identifies opportunities for process improvements. This analysis also examines problems, constraints, and non-value-added activities that occur during a process.

text: Which of the following is considered an advantage of fixed price contracts?
explanation: Cost risk is low for the buyer in FP contracts. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 471 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

choices.text: Risk is shared between buyer and seller.
It is suitable when the scope is not clear.
The buyer can retain control over project resources.

text: Which of the following indicates what is meant by risk propinquity?
explanation: Propinquity is the degree to which a risk is perceived to matter by one or more stakeholders. Where a risk is perceived as very significant, propinquity is high. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 424

choices.text: The degree to which a risk is perceived to matter by one or more stakeholders.
The extent to which the risk is related to other individual project risks.
The period of time that may elapse after a risk has occurred before its impact is discovered.
The period of time before the risk might have an impact on one or more project objectives.

text: You are the project manager of the new electro-mechanical project for your company. You identified risks with project stakeholders and performed qualitative and quantitative risk analysis. You planned risk responses for all top risks. The sponsor asked you to document how and when risk audits will be performed in the project. Where should you document the risk audits policy?
explanation: The project manager is responsible for ensuring that risk audits are performed at an appropriate frequency, as defined in the project’s risk management plan. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 456

text: The value of the daily standup is for:
explanation: Teams run their own standups. When run well, standups can be very useful, provided the nature of the team’s work requires intense collaboration. Make a conscious decision about when the team needs, or can effectively use, standups. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 54

choices.text: The scrum master, to monitor team progress.
The product owner, to know what has been completed from the sprint backlog.
Team members, to keep them apprised of the work within the iteration.
Team leader, to be updated about each team members tasks.

text: You are managing a railroad construction project using traditional approach. One of the stakeholders has discovered a problem in the design, and as a result you have requested a change. The change will cost the project extra two weeks and $10,000 added cost, but without it there will be problems with the final product of the project. What should you do next?
explanation: Review change control procedures, you must first evaluate the impacts of the change on all constraints, then submit it to the Change Control Board. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66

choices.text: Update the project management plan to reflect the change.
Call for a meeting with the stakeholders to figure out whether or not to make the change.
Document the change and its impact, and submit it to the change control board.
Instruct the change control board to approve the change in order not to cause problems to the product.

text: A new medicine has been released in market by a large pharmaceutical company. A serious side effect has been discovered which may cause a sudden decrease in blood pressure. The company has recalled all products from the market. The incurred cost for recalling the medicine is known as:
explanation: Cost of failures found by the customer is considered a cost of non conformance. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 283 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 88

text: During developing the risk management plan for your project, you need to determine the acceptable level of the overall project risk exposure for your project. What should you do first?
explanation: Stakeholder risk appetite should be expressed as measurable risk thresholds around each project objective. These thresholds will determine the acceptable level of overall project risk exposure. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 398 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 122

choices.text: It can be determined from the historical information for a similar project.
It can be determined after performing quantitative analysis for the identified project risks.
It can be determined after planning for risk responses.
Determine the risk appetite for key stakeholders.

text: A project is being implemented using an adaptive approach. During a meeting with the customer, he requested to add a new requirement to the next iteration which might need other resources outside the project team. The needed resources are available within the organization. The project team wants to ensure resources availability to perform the new requirement, however there is a risk that the customer may cancel or postpone the new requirement before the next iteration starts. What should the project team do?
explanation: Soft booking is the term for the process of scheduling resources in a project that haven't been confirmed yet. If the resource or service hasn't yet been confirmed, you have to make assumptions about when they'll be available and what their costs will be. Domain = Process.

choices.text: Inform the customer that they will assign the needed resources and he will pay their fees in case of cancellation.
Use soft booking for the needed resources.
Don't hire the needed resources unless there is a written confirmation from the customer for the new requirement.
Assign two team members to learn the needed skills to perform the new requirements.

text: Bill is the project manager for a software project. He and his team have identified risks, performed qualitative risk analysis and planned responses for top risks. During project executing, an identified risk occured, they have implemented the contingency plan, but it was not effective. What type of plans should they create for implementation, if the selected risk response strategy fails to be effective?
explanation: A fallback plan can be developed for implementation if the selected strategy turns out not to be fully effective or if an accepted risk occurs. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 448

text: A project team is having a weekly meeting. This team meeting during project executing is the best format to :
explanation: Meetings -by definition - is a communication tool. There are other methods to know the project status like sending status reports for example. There are also other methods to make changes to the project and to making team building activities. The project team should use meetings for effective communication. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 376 and 381

text: Which of the following contract types is more suitable for agile projects?
explanation: The goal of agile methods is closer cooperation between the project team and the customer, as expressed in the third Agile Manifesto value, which ranks “Customer collaboration over contract negotiation”. Unlike the traditional triangle of constraints, agile projects attempt to fix time and cost and adjust scope to complete the highest-priority features. From the proposed contracts types time and material will be more suitable for agile projects as the team will be paid according to the completed features within agreed timebox. It might include NTE clause or it can be graduated T&M. However, there are other contract types can be used in agile projects. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 77

text: You are a project manager for a large construction project. The project is to build a new hotel. The project is divided into five phases and it is highly visible in your organization. To ensure formalizing acceptance of the completed project deliverables, you decided to validate scope with the customer to ensure that the project is on track from the customer point of view. When should the Validate Scope process be performed?
explanation: The scope is validated at the end of each phase to ensure that the phase deliverables are accepted by the customer before moving forward to the next phase. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 164

choices.text: At the end of each phase of the project
At the beginning of the project
At the end of the project

text: A project has been initiated using a waterfall approach. The project manager decides to outsource a part of the project work. He is now creating the procurement SOW. The engineering manager wants to describe only the functional characteristics, while the technical support manager wants to add some other items to the procurement SOW. Which of the following best represents what the project manager should do?
explanation: The project adopts waterfall approach. The SOW describes the procurement items in sufficient detail to allow prospective sellers to determine if they are capable of providing the products, services, or results. Sufficient detail can vary based on the nature of the item, the needs of the buyer, or the expected contract form. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 477

choices.text: The procurement SOW should be broad enough, so it doesn’t need to change as the project progresses.
The procurement SOW should be general to allow the seller to make their own decisions.
The procurement SOW should be detailed to allow for clarification later.
The procurement SOW should be as detailed as necessary for the type of project.

text: You are managing a software project to develop a website. You are now in the design phase of the project. The graphic designer on the project recommends a blue background for the new website. When you informed the customer, he rejected and said that he wants the background to be yellow. The graphic designer explained that blue is more comfortable, attractive and calming. A decision must be made today in order not to delay the project schedule. How should this requirements conflict be resolved?
explanation: The project’s success is directly influenced by active stakeholder involvement in the discovery and decomposition of needs into project and product requirements. The customer has high influence on the project, so any conflict should be resolved in favor of the customer. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 140 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 83

choices.text: Compromise between the two options and use green

text: While analyzing the status of the project, the project manager calculated the ratio between earned value EV and planned value PV and found that the ratio is 1.13. He was not satisfied and decided to crash some critical path activities by adding more resources to these activities as a corrective action. Is his decision correct?
explanation: The schedule performance index (SPI) is a measure of schedule efficiency expressed as the ratio of earned value to planned value. An SPI value less than 1.0 indicates less work was completed than was planned. An SPI greater than 1.0 indicates that more work was completed than was planned. The SPI is equal to the ratio of the EV to the PV. Equation: SPI = EV/PV. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 263 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: Yes, his decision is correct. The ratio between EV and PV is called Schedule Performance Index SPI, and if it is more than one, it means that the project is behind schedule. He should compress the schedule.

No, his decision is not correct. The ratio between EV and PV is called Schedule Performance Index SPI, and if it is more than one, it means that the project is behind schedule. He should use fast tracking not crashing.

No, his decision is not correct. The ratio between EV and PV is called Schedule Performance Index SPI, and if it is more than one, it means that the project is ahead of schedule. No need to take corrective action.
Yes, his decision is correct. The ratio between EV and PV is called Schedule Variance SV, and if it is more than one, it means that the project is behind schedule. He should compress the schedule.

text: A government agency contracts with ABC company for development of a new software. The development team consists of project team members from both organizations. The project team members have assumptions, ways of working, and mental models related to how they function within their home organization. In this new project system, which combines the cultures of ABC company and the government agency, what can the project team do to engage, contribute effectively and help to increase the probability that the project system works?
explanation: The project system often brings together a diverse project team engaged in working for a common objective. This diversity brings value to project teams, but they need to consider how to leverage those differences effectively, so that the project team works cohesively. In this new project system, which combines the cultures of two organizations, the project team members can establish a synthesized team culture that creates a common vision, language, and toolset. This can help project team members to engage and contribute effectively and help to increase the probability that the project system works. Domain = Business Environment. The Standard for Project Management, Page 38

choices.text: The project team should mention all the requirements regarding engagement, culture and ways of working in the contract between both organizations.
The project team members can establish a synthesized team culture that creates a common vision, language, and toolset.
The project team from both organizations should work in one common place to enhance communication.
The government agency can enforce their culture and ways of working as they are the customer for this project.

text: A key stakeholder has expressed his concerns about the status reports sent from the project team. He said that he is struggling to obtain the needed information about the project status as the report is overstuffed with unnecessary information. Which of the following is the best course of action for the project manager?
explanation: Reports communicate relevant (usually summary level) information to stakeholders. Often reports are given to stakeholders who are interested in the project status, such as sponsors, business owners, or PMOs. They should be tailored to meet the stakeholders needs. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 385 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 190

choices.text: Inform the stakeholder that the format of the status report has been agreed upon in the communication management plan. If he wants to change it, he can raise a change request.
Ask the stakeholder about his information needs and include them in the status report.
Inform the stakeholder that the status report addresses different stakeholders' needs. It is not tailored according to this particular stakeholder's needs only.
Send a separate report to this particular stakeholder tailored according to his needs.

text: Sandra is a project manager for a software project. During project executing a problem has occurred. The problem was not discussed at all during project planning and it was not added to the risk register. The problem will cause one week delay to the project schedule. Which of the following statements is the best response?
explanation: Management reserves are a specified amount of the project duration withheld for management control purposes and are reserved for unforeseen work that is within scope of the project. Management reserves are intended to address the “unknown-unknowns” that can affect a project. In this situation an unknown unknown has occurred, so you should use management reserve to accommodate it. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 202 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 127

choices.text: Accept the risk and accept the delay because you can not create a response after the problem has occurred.
Add the problem to the risk register and perform qualitative and quantitative risk analysis and create a response if needed.
Ask management to use the management reserve to accommodate for the delay.
Ask for support from your management.

text: Which of the following is the highest richness communication channels that a project team can use to transfer a broad spectrum of information and why?
explanation: Richness in communication allows a broad spectrum of information to be conveyed rapidly. Face-to-face communication is the richest communication channel. It is used to transfer the most information in a given period of time. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 157

choices.text: Videoconferencing, because it allows distributed stakeholders to communicate as if they are co-located.
Face-to-face, because it is used to transfer the most information in a given period of time.
Instant messaging, because it allows stakeholders to communicate urgent information immediately regardless they are co-located or not.
Emails, because it keeps record of all sent and received messages if they are needed later in the project.

text: The fourth value of the Agile Manifesto states that: "Agile teams value responding to change over following a plan". Which of the following agile techniques is closely related to this Agile Manifesto value?
explanation: In agile projects, we know that the initial plans are inadequate, since they are based on insufficient information. So instead of investing effort in trying to bring the project back in line with the original plan, agile teams want to spend more effort and energy responding to the changes that will inevitably arise. In product demos, the team demonstrates all completed work items at the end of the iteration. So team members can get feedback that prevents them from heading in a wrong direction. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55 and 97.

text: Sam is a project manager for a product development project. He is managing his first project. He has finalized the schedule of his project and added all working and non-working days and team vacations on it. He realized that there will be overlapping vacations between his team and the quality control team who will test the product of the project. There will be no one to test the product. Which type of constraints that causes the problem here?
explanation: The constraint here is a resource constraint because the resources will not be available when the project manager needs them. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 10 and 28 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 174

text: Pedro has just been promoted to a project manager position in his organization and he is getting ready to manage his first project. He has been given approved project charter. What is the first thing he should do?
explanation: After issuing the project charter, the project manager must determine which stakeholders have participated in the high level scope. Identify Stakeholders is an initiating process, other processes are planning processes. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 508 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 11-12

choices.text: Confirm which stakeholders had input to the scope
Begin working on a project management plan

text: You are the project manager for a construction project. The project is ahead of schedule and over budget and there are 45 human resources working in the project. The sponsor pulls the plug and cancels the project. What should you do?
explanation: Even if the project is terminated, it must be closed. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 123 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Give the team the day off till you discuss with the sponsor.
Create a budget summary for the remaining unspent budget.
Follow project closure procedures to close the project.
Find new assignments for any people previously assigned to your project.

text: You are managing a project to develop a new pharmaceutical compound. The project is very difficult and has a tight schedule. A quality auditor came to you to perform a quality audit on your project. Your team is already under pressure to complete the project. They object to the audit due to the work pressure. You should explain to the team that the purpose of a quality audit is:
explanation: The project manager is not authorized to check if the customer is following the quality process. Inspecting the deliverables and prevent defect ones to go to the customer is the focus of Control Quality process. The quality audits are tools of the Perform Quality Assurance process which focus on following processes and procedures. Although ISO 9000 is a quality standard, but it is not the main focus of quality audits. So the best answer is to identify inefficient and ineffective policies. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 294

choices.text: To check if the customer is following the quality process.
To inspect deliverables and prevent defective ones to go to the customer.
To satisfy part of an ISO 9000 investigation.
To identify inefficient and ineffective policies.

text: Which of the following is the primary objective of incentive clauses in a contract?
explanation: Incentives and awards may be used to align the objectives of buyer and seller. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 476

choices.text: Reduce risk for the seller by shifting risk to the buyer.

text: Luke is a project manager in a construction company. He was assigned to manage a construction project to build a new shopping center. During performing the strength tests, he found severe failure in the strength tests. He assigned Steve to investigate this problem. Steve started tracking the problem to determine and solve the main reasons behind it. Which tool should Steve use to track the problem?
explanation: Cause-and-effect (Fishbone) diagrams are used to trace the problem's source back to its root cause. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 293

text: Helen is managing a product development project using a traditional approach. A change has occurred on her project. She has documented the change, submitted a change request on the change control system, and submitted that request to the change control board. What's the next thing that must happen on the project?
explanation: After submitting the change to the Change Control Board (CCB), the CCB evaluates the change for approval or rejection using expert judgment. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: She should update the project management plan and project documents to reflect the change.
She should inform the CCB whether or not to approve the change.
Stakeholders on the CCB use expert judgment to evaluate the requested change for approval.
She should meet with the team to analyze the impact of the change on the project's time, scope, and cost.

text: Project teams tailor the appropriate framework that will enable the flexibility to consistently produce positive outcomes within the context of the life cycle of the project. All of the following should be “just enough,” except:
explanation: Tailoring aims to maximize value, manage constraints, and improve performance by using “just enough” processes, methods, templates, and artifacts to achieve the desired outcome from the project. Domain = Process. The Standard for Project Management, Page 44

text: An automobile manufacturer has started a project to build more fuel-efficient cars using a hybrid development approach. The project consists of five sequential phases and each phase has multiple deliverables. The project manager mainly use predictive life cycle with some agile practices. Which agile practices can the project manager recommend to the team to make certain that the deliverables are completed and delivered as expected?
explanation: The definition of done is a checklist of all the criteria required to be met so that a deliverable can be considered ready for customer use. This approach helps ensure that stakeholders agree at the start of the project that the work is not only completed, but a shared understanding of done and the criteria to accept the work are exactly defined. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 85

text: During a construction project, the project manager asked the contractor for a walkthrough of the work site by both of them to ensure a mutual understanding of the work in progress. They are involved in:
explanation: An inspection is a structured review of the work being performed by the contractor. This may involve a simple review of the deliverables or an actual physical review of the work itself. On a construction/engineering/infrastructure project, inspections involve walkthroughs of the site by both the buyer and the contractor to ensure a mutual understanding of the work in progress. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 498

text: Victor is a project manager for a software project. Two team members Kevin and Tom are having a conflict. Kevin explains his opinion, and presents a possible solution. But before Tom starts to explain his opinion, the project manager says, “It is OK, I’ve heard enough, and I’ve decided to go with the solution of Kevin.” This is an example of:
explanation: Here the project manager is pushing his viewpoint at the expense of others; offering only win-lose solutions. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 169

text: According to Alistair Cockburn's Communication Effectiveness model, which of the following is the correct order from highest to lowest for richness of communication channels?
explanation: The richness of different communication channels from lowest to highest is paper, email, audio recording, video recording, IM, phone, face-to-face. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 157

choices.text: Phone, instant messaging, audio recording, paper
Face-to-face, phone, video recording, instant messaging

text: You are the project manager for a large manufacturing project. During estimating activity costs for your project using three-point estimates, a team member asks you that he needs to add 5% to each activity cost to account for unidentified risks. What should you tell him?
explanation: During estimating activity costs, the project team should estimate the activity costs and the contingency reserve, while management reserves are estimated by management as part of the Determine Budget process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 245 and 252

choices.text: He is right, management reserve must be added to account for unidentified risks.
You should instruct him not increase the estimate.
He is right, contingency reserve must be added to account for known-unknowns.
Tell him that you must get sponsor approval before increasing the estimates.

text: Caroline is a project manager for a multi-national project which involves 350 stakeholders from 6 countries. She believs that stakeholder engagement is a key factor for successful projects, so she pays more attention to stakeholder engagement. She is creating a document to help her and her team find the communication gaps with project stakeholders. This document is known as:
explanation: A stakeholder engagement assessment matrix supports comparison between the current engagement levels of stakeholders and the desired engagement levels required for successful project delivery. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 522 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 189

text: The project manager is analyzing project performance using earned value management tool. He determines that the cost variance is – 10,000 $. What is the best thing to do?
explanation: Schedule variance should be determined firstly to complete the data needed to analyze project status and then take the required corrective action. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: Make activities in parallel that are planned in series.
Move resources from the project to one that is not failing.

text: Tom is a newly assigned project manager in agriculture engineering company. He is assigned by Frank, the project sponsor, to manage a new project to produce new organic soil fertilizer. Ronald, the PMO manager, met with both Tom and Frank to discuss the project business documents. Ronald clarified the importance of alignment between the project business documents, project management plan and project charter. Who is responsible for providing recommendations and oversight to keep the project business case, project management plan, project charter and project benefits management plan success measures in alignment with one another?
explanation: The project manager is responsible for providing recommendations and oversight to keep the project business case, project management plan, project charter, and project benefits management plan, success measures in alignment with one another, and with the goals and objectives of the organization. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, page 29.

text: Peter is currently managing a large software project. His project is currently facing chaos. He decides to use directive leadership. What would be the reason behind using this leadership style?
explanation: In moments of chaos, directive action creates more clarity and momentum than collaborative problem solving. Domain = People. The Standard for Project Management, Page 41

choices.text: Because in moments of chaos, directive action creates more participation than any other leadership style.
Because this leadership style elicits more productivity than any other leadership style.
Because this might be the only leadership style he has experience with.
Because in moments of chaos, directive action creates more clarity and momentum than collaborative problem solving.

text: A new organization has implemented few projects and outcomes were successfully delivered to the customers. The organization's PMO wants to evaluate the degree of customers' satisfaction with the products and the projects and whether those customers are willing to recommend a product or service to others. Which of the following metrics can they use?
explanation: A Net Promoter Score measures the degree to which a stakeholder is willing to recommend a product or service to others. It is true that turnover and mood chart are metrics that can be used to measure stakeholders satisfaction, but they can't be used to measure the degree to which a stakeholder is willing to recommend a product or service to others. They are more suitable for internal stakeholders.

text: Bill has just been promoted to a project manager position. It is the first time he manages a project. He is being mentored by Helena, an experienced project manager. Helena advised him that his team members should be involved in developing project schedule and they should approve the final project schedule. Which of the following best describes why he needs the team to approve the final project schedule?
explanation: Team approval to ensure activities can be completed as scheduled. Team approval doesn’t relate to enhancing team buy in or loyalty. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 207

choices.text: To enhance team buy-in and loyalty.
To ensure activities can be completed as scheduled.

text: Midway through the project you are managing, a functional manager has pulled four resources from your project to be assigned to another more important project in the organization. This action has affected your project badly, as it is behind schedule due to the delayed tasks assigned to those resources. After negotiation, you reach an agreement that the functional manager will assign two resources for your project and another two resources will be assigned later after hiring process is complete. Which conflict resolution technique did you use in this situation?
explanation: Compromise/reconcile is searching for solutions that bring some degree of satisfaction to all parties in order to temporarily or partially resolve the conflict. This approach occasionally results in a lose-lose situation. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 349 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 168

text: You are a project manager in a large organization. Recently, your organization is facing a financial crisis, and there was a mass layoff for many employees in the organization. Many projects were affected because of this layoff. Your project is one of the affected projects, because almost 30% of the project resources were released due to the layoff. To deal with the limited number of resources, you adjust the start and finish dates to balance between the demand and available supply of resources. What is the technique you used?
explanation: Resource leveling is a technique in which start and finish dates are adjusted based on resource constraints with the goal of balancing the demand for resources with the available supply. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 211

text: Alberto is managing a large industrial project that is budgeted at 9,000,000$ and scheduled to be completed in 9 months. After 5 months during project executing, he reviews a weekly work performance report from his team. He planned with his team a contingency plan with a predetermined risk trigger for a cost performance index CPI of 0.9. He finds that the earned value measurements are PV = 5,500,000$, EV = 4,850,000$ and AC = 5,250,000$. What should he do first?
explanation: The risk trigger here is when CPI reaches value of 0.9. and here CPI = EV/AC = 0.92 which means that the trigger is not reached. So the risk didn't occur to implement the contingency plan. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 450

choices.text: Look for ways to bring the project back on track.
Determine the root cause of low cost performance index.
Nothing, because the CPI indicates that the trigger is not reached.

text: Which of the following is a basic rule for creating a work breakdown structure?
explanation: The WBS is a hierarchical decomposition of the total scope of work. Each descending level of the WBS represents an increasingly detailed definition of the project work. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 161 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 84

choices.text: Each level of a work breakdown structure is a smaller segment of the level above.
It should list the relationships between different activities.
Each level of a work breakdown structure is a larger segment of the level above.
The planned work is contained within the highest level of WBS components.

text: The buyer's and the seller's project managers are meeting together to discuss the seller's performance to date on this important product development project. There have been three changes to the project caused by a change in laws in the country were the project is being performed and four errors by the seller that are being discussed. What is the best output of such a meeting?
explanation: This is example of a performance review during the Control Procurement process Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 489 and 499 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 77

text: A large software project has been initiated in an organization using an adaptive approach. The project is very large and there are multiple teams working on it. The project has multiple releases and each release has multiple iterations with many features. The project teams sometimes get lost and lack a holistic view of what they are building and why. Which of the following artifacts can help them in this situation?
explanation: A story map is a visual model of all the features and functionality desired for a given product, created to give the project team a holistic view of what they are building and why. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 190

text: The finance department in ABC organization measures the profit and loss for all the projects that are being implemented in the organization. They measured the cost-benefit ratio of one of the projects over several months and find it always greater than 1. They communicated these measures to the project steering committee. Which of the following is not a correct decision for steering committee?
explanation: The cost-benefit ratio is used to determine if the costs of a project outweigh its benefits. If the costs are greater than the benefits, the result will be greater than 1.0. In this case, the project should not be considered unless there are regulatory, social good, or other reasons to do the project. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 102

choices.text: The steering committee decides to complete the project due to regulatory compliance.
The steering committee decides to complete the project to achieve intended profit.
The steering committee decides to complete the project due to social need.
The steering committee decides to start project closure procedures.

text: Philip is the managing director of a famous fast food restaurants chain. He is facing troubles with delaying customer orders. During the last three months, his company received more than 200 complains from customers due to delayed orders. In order to improve the customers' orders management, he decided to treat each order as a project. The project manager will be responsible for handling customers' orders, ensuring order delivery within pre- determined time, resolving customer issues. The project manager is not required to prepare a plan for each order rather than preparing a daily report for all orders. How would you describe this situation?
explanation: The mentioned work here is operational work not project work. An ongoing work effort is generally a repetitive process that follows an organization’s existing procedures. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 16

choices.text: This is a group of related projects, so these orders are considered as a program.
Each order is a project, so there should be a charter for each project.
This is a portfolio, because all these orders help to achieve the same strategic objective.

text: Nathalie is managing a product development project. During the last project status meeting, she displayed the earned value measurements to the project team and project sponsor. SPI = 1.09 and CPI = 0.79. It is clear that the project is running over budget. The project sponsor is very angry and requested from her to share with him the cost incurred on a weekly basis. Which process should she undergo to provide the needed information to the project sponsor?
explanation: Project communications artifacts may include cost incurred. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 387

text: During break time, the customer meets a project team member and they talk about the project, its status, progress, etc. The team member informs the customer that the project will face some delays and two milestones will be missed due to changing project requirements. The project manager hears about this conversation from the customer. What should the project manager do?
explanation: The most important thing is to assess the new requirements through a formal change request and uncover the impact to schedule and budget. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Reprimand the team member and check available resources to consider replacing him.
Submit a change request to reflect new requirements.
Inform the customer that all information related to project progress should be communicated by the project manager only.
Ask team members not to talk to the customer again, unless permission is taken from the project manager.

text: In an adaptive environment, the team knows the acceptance criteria and needs to work backward to develop the code. This is known as:
explanation: TDD stands for Test Driven Development. In this software development technique, we create the test cases first and then write the code underlying those test cases. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 56

text: During planning a construction project, you asked project team members to pay attention to secondary risks. A new team member who has never worked on project before asked you what is meant by secondary risks, you answered him that secondary risks are:
explanation: The secondary risks are risks that arise as a direct result of implementing a risk response. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 439 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 125

choices.text: Discovered during the Identify Risks process.
New risks created by the risk response strategies selected.
Risks that remain after the Plan Risk Responses process.
Lower priority or low impact risks not requiring mitigation efforts.

text: Sam is managing a project to develop a new billing system for an organization. He has finished collecting project requirements when it was discovered that the finance manager requirements were not collected. Which of the following documents is outdated and caused this issue?
explanation: It is clear that the finance manager was not added to the stakeholder register that is why his requirements were missed. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 41 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 12

text: In the previous iterations, the team velocity was 30, 35, 36, 40 and 39 respectively. There are 260 story points remaining in the product backlog. How many more iterations will be needed to complete the product backlog?
explanation: You first need to calculate the average number of points per iteration (velocity) = (30+35+36+40+39) / 5= 180 / 5 = 36. You then divide the remaining work by the average points per iteration (velocity) = 260 / 36 = 7.2. So 8 iterations are needed. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 61

text: Seller A and Seller B believe that their chance of winning work from a buyer will be enhanced if they join forces for one procurement, so, they sign a teaming agreement with each other to address the legal and business aspects of the agreement. Which risk response strategy best describes a teaming agreement?
explanation: Seller A and seller B formed a teaming agreement or joint venture which is an example of sharing an opportunity. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 444 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 125

text: A project manager is leading a large project for electrical system implementation. The project has a large team consisting of people from many functional areas of the company. What is the best power to get cooperation of the team?
explanation: Rewards bring the team's objectives in line with project. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 63

text: A company is adopting transformation from waterfall to agile. Catherine is a project manager in this organization who has been assigned an agile coach role. Which of the following tasks will not be one of her tasks in the new position?
explanation: It is the team coach/scrum master who acts as a servant leader. The tasks of the servant leader includes removing obstacles, providing encouragement and support to the team, shielding the team from interruptions and doing administrative activities for the team. Domain = Process.

text: You are identifying stakeholders at the beginning of your project. You need to classify them in order to perform stakeholder analysis. You grouped the stakeholders based on their active involvement in the project and their ability to effect changes to the project’s planning or execution. Which classification model did you use?
explanation: Impact/influence grid involves grouping the stakeholders based on their active involvement (“influence”) in the project and their ability to effect changes to the project’s planning or execution (“impact”). Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 512

text: Which of the following statements is true regarding project resources management?
explanation: Project resources management is concerned with managing physical and human resources. There is some overlap between Project Resource Management and Project Stakeholder Management. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 309 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 186

choices.text: Project resources management is concerned with managing physical resources, while project stakeholders management is concerned with managing human resources.
Project resources management is concerned with managing human resources, while project scope management is concerned with managing physical resources.
Project resource management is concerned with managing human resources only.
Project resources management is concerned with managing physical and human resources.

text: You are a project manager for a hardware design project. The project is going well until the research and development manager discovered that two of his staff members were working on different versions of the software driver of the hardware, a product of your project. Which of the following best describes what needs the attention of the project manager on this project?
explanation: Configuration management involves making sure that everyone is working off the same documents. It describes how information about the items of the project will be recorded and updated so that the product, service or result of the project remains consistent. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 88

text: You are managing a software project. The project budget seems to be inaccurate. The SPI is 1.15. Two project team members resigned last week and The team has had negative deviation from the activity attributes. What is the best thing for the project manager to do?
explanation: Re-estimating activity attributes is not necessary. The first thing is to calculate SPI and CPI for the project to gain full understanding of the project status. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: Re-compute the project schedule and budget.
Try to deal with the high turn-over rate in your project.

text: The construction manager complains that the project didn’t increase his team’s knowledge of AutoCAD and So, the project is not complete. The customer has formally accepted the project. All stated requirements have been met and there is no evidence that learning AutoCAD was a requirement. The project manager should:
explanation: Formal acceptance of the product and satisfying all documented requirements means end of project work and beginning of project closure. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 123 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Re-plan the project to include the new requirement.
Meet with the construction manager to help resolve the issue.
Meet with the team to discuss the issue and find a solution.

text: A software organization has decided to adopt agile approach. In a large new project, they are struggling to coordinate between multiple scrum teams. Which of the following is the best approach to solve this problem?
explanation: As Scrum projects get larger, multiple Scrum teams might need to coordinate their work.To do this, they commonly use an approach called “scrum of scrums.” As the name suggests, this is a meeting in which a representative from each team reports their progress to the representatives of the other teams. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 111

text: A project manager of a manufacturing project is analyzing activities that doesn’t add value to the project and wastes company resources and time. A team member informed him that a process is not understood and another one informed him that two processes he works on, he thinks they are not needed. The project manager must be leading which of the following?
explanation: Manage Quality process is concerned with following and improving processes and increasing its efficiency. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 290

choices.text: A process rather than a project

text: A sub-contractor in your project went out of business and you need to start construction tomorrow to meet the next milestone date. Which type of contract would you sign with the new sub-contractor?
explanation: Time and material contracts are the fastest and easiest in negotiation. As you don't have time to negotiate a new contract, it is better to select a time and material contract. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 472 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

text: During project planning, a mechanical engineer informed the project manager that the life cycle cost of the new machine is higher than the life cycle cost of another machine. In light of this, what should the project manager do?
explanation: Generally, the product with lower life cycle cost is preferable to be selected. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 245

choices.text: Select the product with the lower life cycle cost.
Select the product with the cheaper price.
Use the contingency reserve to purchase the other machine.
Select the product with the lowest maintenance cost.

text: A project manager is concerned about sending progress reports. Another project manager advised him that he should also watch what is going on in his project, ask about problems, and help team members. Which of the following is the biggest disadvantage of progress reports as compared to watching what is going on in a project, asking questions, and assisting team members?
explanation: Sending progress reports is important, but not like effective project management like watching what is happening, asking about problems and helping team members. Usually project reports are sent one or two weeks later after performing actual work, so reports shows problems after they have occurred. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 385 and 392

choices.text: Progress reports supply information about trends.
Progress reports are not detailed enough.
Progress reports require expending time gathering information from stakeholders.
Progress reports generally show problems after they have occurred.

text: During an industrial project, two team members are in conflict because of difference in opinions concerning the correct order of steps to test the final product of the project. Who is initially responsible for resolving this conflict?
explanation: Project team members are initially responsible for resolving it. If conflict escalates, the project manager should help facilitate a satisfactory resolution using suitable conflict resolution technique. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 348 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 168

text: Rights and obligations related to procurement audits should be described in:
explanation: Rights and obligations related to procurement audits should be described in the procurement contract. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 498 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 75

text: An industrial organization is going to perform an infrastructure project. The project is very large and involves rigorous safety requirements. Which of the following approaches will most fit this project?
explanation: Products that have rigorous safety requirements often use a predictive approach as there is a need for significant up-front planning to ensure that all the safety requirements are identified, planned for, created, integrated, and tested. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 40

choices.text: There is not enough information to decide.

text: You have been working on a construction project for 5 months. The project is on schedule and budget when one of the functional managers tells you the resources committed to the project are no longer available. They will join another project. The new project isn’t critical, but the project manager is the brother of a board member. What is the best course of action in this situation?
explanation: It is the responsibility of the PMO to officially prioritize projects in organizations. While determining when resources become available is not a proactive solution. Using reserves is for identified risks only. Negotiating a new schedule is not a good solution. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 48

choices.text: Negotiate a new schedule with the other project manager.
Use the reserve to hire contractors to complete the work.
Determine when resources will become available.
Ask the PMO to formally prioritize projects.

text: Which of the following is the best definition of WIP limits?
explanation: Limit WIP (work in progress) means restricting the amount of work in progress in a particular state which improves productivity, increases the visibility of issues and bottlenecks, and facilitates continuous improvement. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 25

choices.text: is the maximum number of features in each release.
is the maximum number of iterations in each release.
is the maximum number of items that can be in a particular state.
is the maximum number of user stories in each iteration.

text: Peter is the project manager for an industrial project. The design work of his project is outsourced to another company under a contract. When can he use formal written correspondence with this company?
explanation: The procurement management process is a formal process. All communications must be formal written when there is a change to document the change and sign it from both parties. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 496 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 13

choices.text: When he needs information from the company.
When there is conversation with the company.
When there is a meeting with the company's management.
When there is a change to the procurement statement of work.

text: A change has been made to the project which is being implemented in a predictive environment. What should you do next?
explanation: The change has been already made; the next step is to update the project management plan and project documents. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Prevent the root cause of change.
Revise the project management plan and/or project documents.

text: Which of the following relationships is the least used in Precedence Diagramming Method?
explanation: Start to Finish relationships means that the next task can't be completed until the preceding task starts, which is not commonly used. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 190 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

text: Which of the following statements regarding risk is correct?
explanation: Positive and negative risks are commonly referred to as opportunities and threats. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 397 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 53

choices.text: Risks may have positive or negative impact on project objectives, but all opportunities are risks.
Risks may have positive or negative impact on project objectives, but all risks are issues.
Risks may have positive or negative impact on project objectives, but all risks are threats.
Risks may have positive or negative impact on project objectives, but all risks are opportunities.

text: A Scrum Master is working on a critical task when a team member approached him for help. What should the Scrum Master do?
explanation: Scrum Master's main duties are coaching and guiding the team as he is a servant leader. Scrum master duties always come first - before any other task he is working on. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 41

choices.text: First complete the critical task then help the team member.
First help the team member then complete the critical task.
Complete the critical task and ask another senior team member to help the team member.
Ask the team member to refer to the product owner.

text: Carol is managing a network installation project for a customer that is part of a program. The new network should be installed in 50 different sites located in the same country. She provides a weekly report containing the RAG status of each site to the customer. The customer has drilled through the details of each site behind marked as red or amber and finds that for the same reasons some sites are marked as red and some are marked as amber. Which of the following best describes the issue and the resolution?
explanation: Ambiguity is a state of being unclear, having difficulty in identifying the cause of events, or having multiple options from which to choose. The situation here involves an ambiguity category known as conceptual ambiguity- the lack of effective understanding—occurs when people use similar terms or arguments in different ways. Ambiguity of this type can be reduced by formally establishing common rules and definitions of terms. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 117

choices.text: It is conceptual ambiguity and it can be reduced by formally establishing common definitions of terms.
It is situational ambiguity and it can be reduced by formally establishing common definitions of terms.
It is conceptual ambiguity and it can be reduced by progressive elaboration.
It is situational ambiguity and it can be reduced by progressive elaboration.

text: Peter is a product owner for a software project that is being implemented using agile methodology. During an iteration review, the team demonstrates theA software project that is being implemented using agile methodology. During an iteration review, the team demonstrates the completed product increment during the last iteration. He rejects one of the product features that the development team demonstrated. What should happen next?
explanation: As the feature is rejected, so it means that it does not meet the definition of done or there is a difference between what was done and what was expected. So, it is needed to close this gap by re-writing the related user stories. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: The team should try to convince the product owner to accept the feature.
The related user stories should be added to the next iteration backlog.
The team should re-write the related user stories to address the rejection reason.
The related user stories are returned back to the product backlog for re-prioritization.

text: Sam has just been promoted to a project manager position and he is managing his first major network rollout project. His sponsor is worried about the status of the project . In order to reduce the boss's stress he calculated the earned value management calculations and offered to the sponsor. The project has a total budget of $80,000 and CPI of 0.95. The project has spent $25,000 of its budget so far. The sponsor needs to know how much more money does Sam plan to spend on the project?
explanation: EAC = BAC / CPI=84,210, ETC = EAC -AC=59210 Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 104

text: You have been assigned to manage a software project that should be completed within three months. A project charter has been created.
