You met with the customer, sponsor and key stakeholders to collect requirements and developed a project scope statement. A project management plan has been developed and approved by key stakeholders. You met with the actual user who will use the product of the project to demonstrate how the product of the project will work and discovered that the user's requirements are different from the ones you were initially given and approved by key stakeholders. What should you do?
explanation: You must collect new requirements of the user which will lead to changing parts of the project management plan through change request. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 168 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 83

choices.text: Complete the work as originally planned.
Re-plan the project to include the new requirements.
Try to meet other users to collect other requirements.
Create a change request for the user's requirements.

text: You are managing an accounting project when a new financial manager is hired at your company. He will be affected by all accounting projects in your company. What’s the best thing for you to do?
explanation: The manager is affected by your project, that means he’s a stakeholder, so you should get the new stakeholder’s opinion incorporated in the project. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 11

choices.text: Show him the project charter so that he knows that you are in charge of the project.
Work with him to understand the current requirements and determine if he has new ones to add to the project.
Add him to the communications management plan.

text: You are managing a project for a customer. The customer has stated in the contracted that there will be a 5% of the project budget as a bonus if the project team delivers the project one month earlier than the planned project completion date. Which of the following can you do to exploit this opportunity?
explanation: Crashing is a technique used to shorten the schedule duration for the least incremental cost by adding resources to critical path activities. This will increase the probability of completing the project earlier than planned. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

text: A project team has created a software as a project deliverable for a customer. The customer has initiated the project and requested a software solution because they think that the solution resolves the business need for higher productivity. The software which is the output of the project does not enable the productivity outcome that is intended. The project stakeholders feel that the project has failed. Which of the following best represents the root cause of this issue?
explanation: The main issue here is the focus on deliverables not on the intended outcomes. To support value realization from projects, project teams shift focus from deliverables to the intended outcomes. Doing so allows project teams to deliver on the vision or purpose of the project, rather than simply creating a specific deliverable. Domain = Business Environment. The Standard for Project Management, Page 36

choices.text: The project team did not understand the intended outcome from the deliverable.
The project requirements were not collected in details at the beginning of the project.
The project team were not involved from the beginning of the project.
There was a communication gap between the customer and the project team.

text: Susan is a project manager for a new software project. This type of projects has not been previously done before by her company and she has never managed a similar project to this one. What is the best method of estimating the time for activities that have not previously been done by her company?
explanation: Parametric and analogous estimating techniques depend on historical information, so they won’t be used because it is the first project for her company, Monte Carlo analysis is not used for estimating activity durations. Three point estimation will be the best choice in this case. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 178

text: The sixth principle of the Agile Manifesto states that: "The most efficient and effective method of conveying information to and within a development team is face-to-face conversation". Which of the following agile practices and techniques is closely related to this principle?
explanation: Face-to-face communication allows individuals to quickly transfer alot of information in a richer way that includes emotions and body language. Teams use standups to microcommit to each other, uncover problems, and ensure the work flows smoothly through the team. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide, Page 53 and 80

text: Catherine is a project manager for a new product development project. After the product of the project has been released, it was found to have numerous features more than any other similar product in the market, but obvious defects have been found by the users of the product. The product is categorized as:
explanation: The high-grade product has numerous features and the low quality product has many defects. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 274

text: The project team is in the design phase of a new car prototype. The team is analyzing which type of motors and wheels would provide a high speed with more safety. Which of the following is the most likely technique would they use to determine these variables?
explanation: Design of experiments helps to determine a group of variables to improve quality. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 290

text: Which of the following activities is closely related to Agile Principle 2: "Welcome changing requirements, even late in development. Agile processes harness change for the customer's competitive advantage"?
explanation: As expressed in Agile Manifesto Principle 2, staying flexible to meet the customer's changing business needs is fundamental to agile, and the backlog is one of the key ways that agile teams ensure this adaptability. The backlog is always evolving, and it needs to be kept continually updated with the latest requirements and new information. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8.

text: Smith is managing a large product development project. The customer of the project has very tight quality tolerance and will not accept any defected product. He has set strict quality control measures and feedback verification loops from a similar previous project. However during scope validation, the customer has rejected a high percentage of the products due to defects found. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: It is clear that in this situation the quality control procedures including verification and feedback loops were taken from a similar project without tailoring. Quality control procedures and quality assurance steps need to be tailored for the specific needs for this project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 151

choices.text: Escalate this topic to the steering committee.
More tailoring for quality control procedures and quality assurance steps.
Demonstrate the quality control measures and feedback verification loops to the customer.

text: You are managing a risky construction project. Your organization has a bad history of dealing with risky projects and none of your team members has experience in working with this type of projects. You decide to acquire a risk management expert to help you reduce the overall project risk to be below the acceptable risk threshold. Which type of contract is most appropriate for your organization to sign with the expert?
explanation: Time and material contracts are often used for staff augmentation, acquisition of experts, and any outside support when a precise statement of work cannot be quickly prescribed. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 472 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

text: A large electronics organization has initiated a project to develop a new gaming device. The initial schedule to develop the new device with a set of pre-defined features is 6 months. Another competitor has launched a similar product with the same features. The project team has increased the number of features to stay relevant with the market. This pushes the launch date two more months. This is known as:
explanation: In this situation, the definition of the project goal being delivered or "done" is constantly moving. Project teams track the planned rate of project goal achievement relative to the rate of progress toward completion. The longer the project takes to complete, the further the project goal of "done" is likely to move. This is sometimes referred to as "done drift." Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 85

text: You are a project manager for an electrical system implementation project using traditional approach. A team member approaches you with a change that could cut your schedule down by two weeks. What is the first thing you should do?
explanation: It’s important to check how the project will impact the other two constraints. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66

choices.text: Write up a change request and submit it to the change control board.
Make the change ASAP; it’s going to save time and nobody will want the project to take longer than it should.
Figure out the impact on the scope of the work and the cost before you write up the change request.
Tell the team member that you’ve already communicated the deadline for the project, so you can’t make any changes now.

text: The organization has 4 projects to select one of them for implementation. Project A has NPV $50,000 and payback period of 2 years. Project B has NPV of $30,000 and payback period of 6 months. Project C has NPV of $40,000 and payback period of 1 year. Project D has NPV of 35000$ and payback period of 18 months. Based on this data which project will the organization select to implement, if they want to achieve profit in the shortest time period?
explanation: If the organization wants to achieve profit in the shortest time period, so the project with the shortest payback period is selected. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 102

text: Physical resource management is concentrated in all of the following except:
explanation: Identifying roles and responsibilities of team members clearly without ambiguity is parts of managing human resources not physical resources. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 310 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 65

choices.text: Identifying roles and responsibilities of team members clearly without ambiguity.
Securing critical equipment and material on time.

text: Matt is an agile practitioner working on a software project. Midway through the project, the team tells him that they need feedback from some key subject matter experts who aren't assigned to the project. How should Matt respond?
explanation: During product demonstration, the team demonstrates all completed work items at the end of the iteration. SME's can be invited to provide their feedback. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 55

choices.text: Ask the sponsor to assign more full-time subject matter experts to the project.
Remind the team that they can get all the feedback they need from the product owner, instead of going outside the project.
Invite those subject matter experts to participate in the team's product demo meetings and provide their feedback.
Ask the team to proceed, since they have all the expertise needed to complete the project.

text: A cyber security project is being implemented to protect ABC company's data. During project execution, a new law is being discussed which requires additional protection for the user's information. This will require additional security features to be added which were not part of the original scope. What should the project manager do in this situation?
explanation: The law has not yet been applied, but there is a risk that new requirements will be added to the project scope to comply with the new law. So, the best answer is to add a risk for the new requirements that might emerge as a result of the new law and develop a response plan. Domain = Business Environment. The Standard for Project Management, Page 27

choices.text: Hold a meeting with the legal department to learn about the new requirements.
Raise a change request to include the new needed features for additional data protection.
Do nothing until the law is applied.
Add a risk to the risk log and develop a response plan.

text: Everybody in the company does what Paul says, because they all know that he is a close friend to the CEO of the company. What kind of power does he hold over the team?
explanation: Everybody in the company does what Paul says not because his power or his authority but because of his relationship with the CEO. The power used here is referent power. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 63

text: You are managing a construction project. Four sellers have sent proposals as a response to the RFP that the procurement department in your organization sent. You need to gain a more objective understanding of sellers’ capabilities in order to rank their proposals and select the best one. Which technique may best help avoid bias and subjective decisions?
explanation: Weighting system allows the evaluation committee to select the seller by weighting the source according to the selection criteria. The buyer may compare sellers to select the seller who best meets the selection criteria. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 479

text: Samuel is managing a product development project that is bugeted at 1,550,000$. The project is supposed to be completed by today, but it is now 76% complete only. The incurred cost to reach this point is 1,315,000$. What is the cost performance index (CPI) at this point?
explanation: AC=1,315,000$, as the project should be finished at this point, so PV = 1,550,000$, EV =0.761,550,000 = 1,178,000. CPI = EV/AC = 1,178,000/1,315,000 = 0.9. SPI = EV/PV = 1,178,000/1,550,000 = 0.76. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100*

text: An organization wants to adopt agile transformation. The organization management board need to include more agile methodologies in the organization's projects. What should be done prior to including agile methodologies in the organization's projects?
explanation: The organization should first assess the organizational culture and readiness to undertake this initiative. This organization change usually takes time and is recommended to be gradually and incrementally not an abrupt change. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 160

choices.text: Assess the organization's culture and readiness for transformation.
Hire a group of agile coaches.
Provide training for the current employees on agile methodologies.
Pause all current projects because they adopt traditional approach.

text: You are trying to decide whether to contract out a construction job or do it within your company. To do it within your company, you will have to hire an engineer for $35,000 and pay a construction team $15,000 per week. A contractor will cost you a price of $19,000 per week, and your expert agrees that you won’t find a lower price than that. The job will take 16 weeks. What’s the best way to proceed?
explanation: Outsourcing Cost = 19000 * 16 = 304000, House Cost = 15000 * 16 + 35000 = 275000. House cost is less.

choices.text: Pay the contractor to do the job.
Do the work within your company.
Select a fixed price contract if your scope is not clear.

text: A project manager on an industrial project decided to insure against fire. This is an example of:
explanation: Insurance means transference of risk. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123

text: During executing a mechanical project using a waterfall approach, an experienced engineer calls the project manager in private and informs him that there is a simpler design which is more fuel-efficient and it could achieve great cost savings. What should the project manager do?
explanation: It is not the responsibility of the engineer to evaluate impacts of the change on project constraints. A change request should be raised with the engineer's suggestion for the new design and it will be assessed and approved or rejected by the Change Control Board. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Do nothing, the plan has already been approved including the old design.
Ask the engineer to submit a change request.
Ask the engineer to evaluate the impacts on other project constraints.
Hold a meeting with the engineer and the designer of the old system to discover variances.

text: Martin is a project manager for a large construction project. He is having difficulties in keeping the project schedule on track. The project is budgeted at $3,000,000, and has 310 human resources. The project duration is three months and has 380 activities. There are 90 activities on the critical path. The project SPI is 0.78 and CPI is 1.09. Under these circumstances. What should the monthly status report contain?
explanation: Status report contains the status of the project in accordance to the project management plan and possible options. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 385 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 190

choices.text: That the project will need more resources.
That the project is doing well.
That the project will finish after the required completion date.

text: If your organization is using Kaizen and and Just-In-Time (JIT) methods, you must take them into consideration when you manage your projects because they will mostly affect on:
explanation: Due to the scarce nature of critical resources, in some industries, several trends have become popular in the past several years. There is extensive literature about lean management, just-in-time (JIT) manufacturing, Kaizen, total productive maintenance (TPM), Theory of constraints (TOC), and other methods. A Project Manager should determine if the performing organisation has adopted one or more resource management tools and adapt to the project accordingly. Domain = process. PMBOK 6th edition, page 310.

text: You have a project with the following activities: Activity A takes 5 days and should happen after the project starts. Activity B takes 8 days and should happen after Activity A. Activity C must happen after the project starts and takes 4 days. Activity D must happen after activities C and takes 6 days. Activity E must take place after activities B and D and takes 6 days. Activity F takes place after activity D and takes 3 days. Activity G takes place after activity E and takes 10 days. Activities F and G are the last activities of the project. Which of the following is true if activity F actually takes 6 days?
explanation: There are three paths are Start-A-B-E-G-End = 29 days, Start-C-D-E-G-End = 26 days, and Start-C-D-F-End = 13 days. If F = 6 days so path Start-C-D-F-End only changes to 16 days. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 210 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 58-60

choices.text: The critical path will be the same.
The project duration increases by 3 days.
The critical path changes to start, C, D, F, end.

text: Andy is a project manager for a software project. The scope of the project was approved by the customer and key stakeholders at the beginning of the project, but whenever the customer validates deliverables, he comes up with new requirements that he would like to add into the product. Andy is working with the customer to find what requirements were missed in the planning stages of the project and how to plan better in the future. What is the best description of his project’s current situation?
explanation: Scope creep means the uncontrolled expansion to product or project scope without adjustments to time, cost, and resources. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 168 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 87

text: If the project is facing chaos, which of the following leadership styles is most appropriate in this case?
explanation: In moments of chaos, directive action creates more clarity and momentum than collaborative problem solving. Domain = People. The Standard for Project Management, Page 41

text: Dave is assigned to manage a large and complex project under a contract with the government. The project is to replace existing copper cables by optical fiber cables. The project is very important and critical to the organization. The project charter has been approved. Dave and his team start to identify project stakeholders. It is clear that the project involves a large and complex community of stakeholders with different power and influence levels. Dave needs to determine the relative importance of each identified stakeholder. Which of the following categorization methods is the most appropriate to use?
explanation: The salience model is useful for large complex communities of stakeholders or where there are complex networks of relationships within the community. It is also useful in determining the relative importance of the identified stakeholders. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 513 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 171

text: In high-performing projects, who should exhibit effective leadership skills?
explanation: Leadership is not exclusive to any specific role. High-performing projects may feature multiple people exhibiting effective leadership skills, for example, the project manager, sponsors, stakeholders, senior management, or even project team members. Anyone working on a project can demonstrate effective leadership traits, styles, and skills to help the project team perform and deliver.

text: Which of the following situations entails high volatility?
explanation: Volatility exists in an environment that is subject to rapid and unpredictable change. Volatility can occur when there are ongoing fluctuations in available skill sets or materials. Volatility usually impacts cost and schedule. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 122

choices.text: The project team members have never worked on this type of projects before and they lack understanding and awareness about the product of the project.
We have many options and we are not sure which one we can undertake. We need to wait sometime till more information evolves.
It was a tough challenge to link the organization's ticketing system to the customer’s systems due to unpredicted systems behavior.
The customer requested many alternatives to the initially stated requirements which has ended up with a product totally different than the agreed one at the beginning of the project.

text: Mathew is managing a software project to develop a website for a new press agency. The project is very large and involves 350 stakeholders located in 9 countries speaking 5 different languages. Recently, he noticed that misunderstanding occurred between many stakeholders when using the instant messenger software available in the organization's intranet. He asked the IT department to add emotional faces to the instant messenger software. How can these emotional faces help the sender?
explanation: Encoding means that the message is coded into symbols, such as text, sound or some other medium for transmission. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 371

text: Risk owners for individual project risks may be nominated as part of the ________ process, and will be confirmed during the _________ process.
explanation: Risk owners for individual project risks may be nominated as part of the Identify Risks process, and will be confirmed during the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 411

choices.text: Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis and Plan Risk Responses
Identify Risks and Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis
Plan Risk Management and Implement Risk Responses
Identify Risks and Implement Risk Responses

text: Phillip is leading a large software project using adaptive approach. He has been approached by the project sponsor to know the expected project completion date. Phillip has informed him that the likely completion date is after 6 months, because:
explanation: Each two weeks the team completes 50 story points. The remaining story points are 560, so they need 560/50 = 11.2 iterations = 12 iterations (6 months). Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 61

choices.text: The team average velocity is 50 story points per two-weeks iteration and there are 560 story points remaining.
The team average velocity is 60 story points per two-weeks iteration and there are 600 story points remaining.
The team average velocity is 30 story points per one-week iteration and there are 600 story points remaining.
The team average velocity is 25 story points per one-week iteration and there are 500 story points remaining.

text: Chris is a project manager for a construction project. He and his team have finished defining project activities and they need to determine the relationships between project activities to create the network diagram. They decided that the first task is "digging work", it must be completed before the second task "pour foundation" begins. The third task "level concrete" cannot begin until "pour foundation" begins. What is the relationship between the "digging work", "pour foundation" and "level concrete"?
explanation: As digging work must finish before pour foundation can start, so the relationship between digging work and pour foundation is Finish to Start FS. As the level concrete can't start until pour foundation starts, so the relationship between pour foundation and level concrete is Start to Start SS. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 190 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

choices.text: Digging work and pour foundation is Finish to Start FS and pour foundation and level concrete is Finish to Start FS.
Digging work and pour foundation is Finish to Start FS and pour foundation and level concrete is Start to Start SS.
Digging work and pour foundation is Finish to Finish FF and pour foundation and level concrete is Start to Start SS.
Digging work and pour foundation is Start to Start SS and pour foundation and level concrete is Finish to Start FS.

text: James is the project manager of a software project. Midway through his project, he realizes that there’s a problem with a part of the software code and his team members are going to redo this part of the code. One of his team members suggests that the problem is not severe enough to cause all of that re-work. James says that he’s worked on a project that made the same mistake before and they ended up having to redo the work when inspectors discovered it. He convinces the team member that it’s probably better to fix it now than later. What kind of power is he using to make the decision in this situation?
explanation: James is using expert power. Since he’s been through this problem before. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 63

text: You are managing a construction project to build a new factory. You are partway through the project, and your team has just delivered the design of the factory . Your team makes a presentation to the project sponsor and key stakeholders. Later, the sponsor informs you that there is an important representative from the government who must approve the design to give you the license to the new factory, and whose requirements are not being met. As a result, you must now make a large and expensive change to accommodate that representative's requirements. What is the best explanation for this?
explanation: Stakeholder analysis means talking to the stakeholders and figuring out their needs. If there is an important client who has needs that your project is supposed to fulfill, that client is always a stakeholder. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 11-12

choices.text: The government representative should have informed with all his requirements at the beginning of the project.
Stakeholder analysis was not performed adequately.
The team made a serious mistake and you need to use penalty power to correct it.
You do not have enough budget to perform the project.

text: An organization is leading an agile transformation. The first project using agile methodology has been initiated. The project team has never worked on agile projects before. One team team members asks about the project management plan. The agile coach has clarified to him that planning in agile projects is different than traditional projects. Agile projects use adaptive planning. Which Agile Manifesto value is concerned with adaptive planning?
explanation: In agile projects, planning is critical to project success but it is different than traditional projects. Planning is an ongoing process and provides multiple mechanisms to proactively update the plan. It is not one time stand-alone activity. Total planning is more but distributed, adaptive planning means plan to replan and responding to change is a way to uncover requirements

choices.text: Responding to change over following a plan
Individuals and interactions over processes and tools

text: To support value realization from projects, project teams should most focus on:
explanation: To support value realization from projects, project teams shift focus from deliverables to the intended outcomes. Doing so allows project teams to deliver on the vision or purpose of the project, rather than simply creating a specific deliverable. Domain = Business Environment. The Standard for Project Management, Page 36

text: During measuring the project performance against the project management plan, you discover that the project is running behind schedule and the final deliverable will be delayed by one month after the required date by the customer. What should the project manager do?
explanation: Working overtime or requesting to change the project end dates are reactive solutions, even evaluating the impacts of completing the project later is not a good solution. The project manager must be proactive, so evaluating the impacts for crashing or fast tracking is the best solution. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 223 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 58-60

choices.text: Look for options with project team members like adding more resources or performing critical path activities in parallel.
Let the team members work overtime to finish the project on time.
Evaluate the impact of completing the project later than the required date.
Make a change request to extend the project and schedule and submit it to the change control board.

text: A company is moving to a new location and at the same time is adopting transformation from waterfall to agile. What is the most suitable design for team space?
explanation: Companies are designing their offices to balance common and social areas (sometimes called “caves and common”) with quiet areas or private spaces where individuals can work without being interrupted. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 46

choices.text: Aqile teams should work in one room without separators to provide open and collaborative work environment.
Private spaces where individuals can work without being interrupted.
Distributed offices with instant messaging and video conferencing tools.

text: During the execution of your new product development project, a new quality standard is issued and your project team is holding a meeting with the quality department to determine how the standard will apply to the running project. This is part of which quality management process?
explanation: Team is defining how to meet the new standard, it is part of the Plan Quality Management process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 277

text: The eighth principle of the Agile Manifesto states that: “Agile processes promote sustainable development. The sponsors, developers, and users should be able to maintain a constant pace indefinitely”. Which of the following agile practices and techniques is closely related to this principle?
explanation: Agile methods recognize the value of a sustainable pace that allows team members to maintain a work-life balance. Allowing the team to create their own team charter with team norms and values will help create an agile environment in which team members can work to the best of their ability as a team. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide, Page 50 and 80

text: A project team are working on an product development project in an industrial environment using Kanban system. They are developing the project schedule. The project activities are relatively similar in size and scope and can be bundled by size and scope. The project manager needs to limit a team’s work in progress in order to balance demand against the team’s delivery throughput. Which scheduling method is the most appropriate to use?
explanation: On-demand scheduling. This approach, typically used in a Kanban system, is based on the theory-of-constraints and pull-based scheduling concepts from lean manufacturing to limit a team’s work in progress in order to balance demand against the team’s delivery throughput. It is often used for projects that evolve the product incrementally in operational or sustainment environments, and where tasks may be made relatively similar in size and scope or can be bundled by size and scope. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 177

text: The testing team members always complain that they are overloaded and they are always requested to do more work than they can handle. What is the best way to handle this issue?
explanation: Limiting the WIP will address the issue of being overwhelmed with work. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 65

choices.text: Use generalizing specialists who can work as developers and testers.
Add more testers to the testing team.
Use Kanban system and limit the WIP at testing stage.

text: Catherine is closing her first project, she is collecting lessons learned with project team members and stakeholders. Many stakeholders were not satisfied about the project and they complained that their needs were not fully met. Her boss asks her about the steps she took on her project to improve customer satisfaction. Which subsidiary plan would she consult to determine this information?
explanation: The quality management plan is a component of the project management plan that describes how applicable policies, procedures, and guidelines will be implemented to achieve the quality objectives. It describes the activities and resources necessary for the project management team to achieve the quality objectives set for the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 286 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 186

text: During measuring project performance for a product development project against project baselines, you found that the schedule performance index (SPI) of 0.93 and the cost performance index (CPI) of 0.82. Generally, What is the reason behind this?
explanation: The project is behind schedule and over budget. Changes in scope may not result in increasing time and cost. When a supplier go out of business this doesn’t necessarily mean that affected activities are on the critical path. When additional equipments are needed this will increase cost and might not increase time. So the correct choice is that equipment is needed for a critical path activity that was delayed and the team purchased a more expensive one instead. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: A supplier went out of business and a new one needed to be found.
An equipment needed for a critical path activity was delayed and project team purchased a more expensive one instead.
Additional equipment needed to be purchased.

text: Kevin is managing his first project in the organization. The project is a risky one. Kevin is reporting to Charles who is the project sponsor. He consulted Charles because he is certified PMI-RMP risk consultant in the organization to help him in risk planning for the project. How would you categorize Kevin's request?
explanation: Kevin requested support from his manager because of his experience in risk management, so it is considered expert judgement. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 404

text: Which form of communication is appropriate for a project manager to report the final status of the closed contract to the stakeholders?
explanation: These reports must be formal written. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 13

text: Gary is managing a large tele-communications project. The installation work will be outsources to another company. After the prospective sellers have been identified and received the procurement documents, Gary invites the sellers to attend a bidder conference. What should he do?
explanation: The procurement management process requires formality. Ensuring that all prospective sellers got the same information in writing ensures that there is no bias from the project manager to any of them. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 489 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 70

choices.text: To hold separate meetings with each bidder.
All questions and answers are put in writing and sent to all sellers.
All sellers get answers to their questions only.
Negotiate with the potential sellers to get the least possible price.

text: Alexander is a project manager for a major software project. He is interviewing the members of a project team to test their understanding of the assigned risk responses as risk owners. He is working with the risk management department in the organization to evaluate the risk responses to determine their effectiveness in the project. What project management technique is he performing?
explanation: Risk audits examine and document the effectiveness of risk responses in dealing with identified risks and their root causes, as well as the effectiveness of the risk management process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 456

text: In which procurement management process is the source selection criteria created for your project?
explanation: Source selection criteria is created during the Plan Procurement Management process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 478

text: Diana is a project manager for a new IT project. She is working with project key stakeholders to analyze and prioritize requirements. She pays more attention to customer satisfaction because one of the project requirements is to achieve a high-level of customer satisfaction for the product of the project. What is the risk here in project requirements?
explanation: Requirements need to be measurable and customer satisfaction is not measurable. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 147

choices.text: Achieving high-level customer satisfaction is an assumption and it should be documented in the project assumptions log.
Achieving high-level customer satisfaction is an assumption and it should be documented in the project assumptions log.
Achieving high-level customer satisfaction is a constraint and it must be balanced with other constraints like time, scope and cost.
Achieving high-level customer satisfaction is a top risk and it must be identified, analyzed and appropriate response should be planned for it.

text: You are the project manager for a software project. The project sponsor has asked you for a forecast for the cost of project completion. Which of the following is the best metric to use for forecasting?
explanation: Only ETC & VAC look forward (forecast), while CPI, SPI, SV, CV, EV & AC look backward. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 265 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 104

text: The intrinsic motivators include all of the following, except:
explanation: There are three types of intrinsic motivators: autonomy, mastery, and purpose. Salary and rewards are extrinsic motivators. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 159

text: The second value of the Agile Manifesto states that: "Agile teams value working software over comprehensive documentation". Which of the following agile techniques is closely related to this Agile Manifesto value?
explanation: Work in progress (WIP) is the term given to work that has been started but has not yet been completed. Excessive levels of WIP are associated with a number of problems like hiding bottlenecks, representing risk in the form of potential rework, and consuming investment capital and delivering no return. The second principle of Agile Manifesto states that we value working software over comprehensive documentation, so it is closely related to WIP. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide, Page 97

text: The sponsor asks the developers in a scrum team to add a very important item to the current sprint. What should they do?
explanation: The team must inform the product owner first as he is responsible to prioritize items in the backlog. Domain = People.

choices.text: Add the item to the next sprint.
Inform other members of the scrum team so they can take decision.
Add the item to the current sprint.
Cancel the current sprint and start a new sprint including this item.

text: A key stakeholder has approached the project team who are working on a software project using an adaptive approach with a concern. The stakeholder can’t find the features that at intermediate states, such as features designed but not yet constructed, those in quality assurance, or those in testing in any of the project artifacts. To which of the following artifacts can the project team guide him?
explanation: Cumulative flow diagram (CFD) indicates features completed over time, features in development, and those in the backlog. It may also include features at intermediate states, such as features designed but not yet constructed, those in quality assurance, or those in testing. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 188

text: You are managing a project to design a high-efficiency solar cell using a predictive development approach. Midway through the project, several issues have occurred and now the project schedule and budget are in jeopardy. What is the best course of action to address these issues?
explanation: Management reserves are withheld for unforeseen risks that affect the project. According to the scenario, the issues developed during the project were unexpected. All other actions can be performed later in the project. Adding an entry in the issue log will not address those issues. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 254 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 62

choices.text: Add an entry in the issue log.

text: The project manager is holding a risk reassessment meeting with the project team and the project sponsor, the sponsor is looking at the latest list of top risks in the project report. A new risk has been identified and assessed. That risk ranking was lower than all of the previously identified risks. Three fallback plans have been adjusted based on the severity of previous risk impacts. Which of the following has the project manager forgotten to do?
explanation: It is not the responsibility of the project sponsor to review a full list of non-critical risks but he may review residual risks. There is nothing called contingency risks. Quantitative risk analysis is not performed to risks which rank lower than all of the previously identified risks. Quantitative risk analysis is performed for top risks only. The best answer here is to look at the impact of the new risk to the project as a whole as a part of the Identify Risks process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 411 and PMBOK 7th edition Page 123

choices.text: Look at the impact of the new risk to the project as a whole.
Provide the sponsor with the full list of non-critical risks.
Look for contingency risks from the fallback plan.
Perform quantitative risk analysis for the new risk.

text: Carolyne is managing a large project to design a high-efficiency solar cell. The project is very complex and has been running for 18 months. Dan is a team member who has been working very hard and has consistently shown great performance during the project. Carolyne wants to recognize Dan's performance. What should she do?
explanation: People are motivated when they feel they are valued in the organization and this value is demonstrated by the rewards given to them. Generally, money is viewed as a tangible aspect of any reward system, but intangible rewards could be equally or even more effective.

choices.text: Provide a reward that aligns with his personal goals.
Provide a certificate of appreciation in the next team meeting.
Send him a thanks email and keep his functional manager in copy.
Ask his functional manager to approve a bonus for him.

text: Management reserves are often viewed as the part of the budget intended to address:
explanation: Management reserves are an amount of the project budget withheld for management control purposes and are reserved for unforeseen work that is within scope of the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 252 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 62

choices.text: Unanticipated events that are within scope of the project
Unanticipated events that are not within scope of the project

text: A software project has been initiated in an adaptive environment. The team decided to build the demo first and add features to the initial product. This is known as:
explanation: Continuous Delivery is the practice of delivering feature increments immediately to customers, often through the use of small batches of work and automation technology. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 151

text: Who is responsible to ensure that all procurements meet the needs of the project?
explanation: It is the project management team’s responsibility to make certain that all procurements meet the specific needs of the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 461

choices.text: Both the project manager and the procurement manager

text: A project has been initiated to build a new factory in a city. The project manager has obtained the necessary approvals and government permits for building the factory. When the project execution has started, it was found that the neighbours went to the location and object to building a factory in this location. What should the project manager do to move forward with the project?
explanation: Stakeholders are the people, groups, or organizations that could impact or be impacted by the project. Neighbours here are stakeholders who may impact the project negatively. They are resistant to the project. The project manager should discuss with them the project objectives, understand their requirements and try to increase their engagement level and move them from resistant stakeholders to neutral stakeholders. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 521 and The Standard for Project Management, Page 32

choices.text: Add a risk to the risk register for neighbours resistance and document its negative impact on the project progress.
Disregard the stakeholders requirements and continue building the factory.
Meet with the neighbours, explain the project objectives and discuss their requirements.

text: A project team has just been formed to work on a new software project. Which of the following is the most appropriate leadership style for the project manager at this stage in the project?
explanation: Early in a team's formation, the role of the team leader is to directly help with project activities and present a clear, tactical picture of what needs to be done. So the directing leadership style is more directive than supportive. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 30

text: You are a project manager for a product development project which uses a traditional life cycle. A team member approaches you with a change that could cut your schedule down by one week and the change is easy to make. What is the first thing you should do?
explanation: Review change control procedures; you must first evaluate the impacts of the change on all constraints. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Write up a change request and submit it to the change control board.
Make the change; it’s going to save time and nobody will want the project to take longer than it should.
Figure out the impact on the scope of the work and the cost before you write up the change request.
Tell the team member that you’ve already communicated the deadline for the project, so you can’t make any changes now.

text: Midway during a sprint, the customer representative keeps calling the development team asking them to add a key feature to the current sprint. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: The scrum master/agile coach is a servant leader who has the skills of facilitation, coaching, and impediment removal. He is also responsible for ensuring that scrum methodology is being followed and applied effectively and he keeps the team and stakeholders focused on learning and using agile processes. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 41

choices.text: The development team should escalate this issue to the product owner.
The agile coach should talk to the customer representative and explain how agile projects work.
The team should ask the customer representative not to disturb them anymore during the sprint.
The team should reject the customer request for adding any new features during the sprint.

text: Sergio is a newly assigned project manager in a construction firm. He has been assigned to establish a PMO in the organization, to provide the projects' team members with required templates, training, access to information and lessons learned. According the mentioned degree of control, the PMO is considered to be:
explanation: Supportive PMOs provide a consultative role to projects by supplying templates, best practices, training, access to information and lessons learned from other projects. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 48

text: Mark is a project manager for a tele-communications project in his organization. Tom is his sponsor and he is working with David, the risk manager in the organization to determine the effectiveness of the risk responses. Tom and David are concerned that some of the risks assigned to Frederick, a project team member to implement the planned risk responses may not be addressed to the depth they would like. In this scenario, what are they doing?
explanation: Risk audits examine and document the effectiveness of risk responses in dealing with identified risks and their root causes, as well as the effectiveness of the risk management process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 456

text: You are a project manager for a project which is facing the following risks: 20% chance a supplier will not deliver the needed spare parts on time, creating an additional cost of $30,000; 10% chance product may need rework adding a cost of $12,000; 25% chance the project will finish earlier than planned, so saving lease cost of $8,000. What is the expected monetary value of these risks?
explanation: EMV = 0.2*30,000+0.1*12,000-0.25*8,000 Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 435 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 176*

text: Sergio is the project manager for an IT project. He receives a phone call from Harold, a risk owner, who informed him that an identified threat has occurred. Harold implemented the contingency plan but it was inadequate. Which of the following is the best response for Sergio?
explanation: A fallback plan can be developed for implementation if the selected strategy turns out not to be fully effective. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 448

choices.text: Inform Harold to simply accept the risk.
Inform Harold to implement the fallback plan.
Inform Harold to implement a workaround.
Inform Harold to assess the impact of the risk.

text: Samuel has been assigned an industrial project to manage. This is his first project to manage. The project is $100,000 over budget. The project team has completed $500,000 worth of work. The project sponsor is worried about the status of the project, he asks Samuel to provide a quick report about the status of the project. Which of the following statements would he mention in his report?
explanation: EV=500,000 and CV=-100,000, AC=600,000. CPI = EV/AC Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 262-263 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

text: A project team has been formed to work on a software project which has just been initiated. It is the first time for this team to work together. Which of the following is the best way to lead this team in this stage of the project?
explanation: Early in a team's formation, the role of the team leader is to directly help with project activities and present a clear, tactical picture of what needs to be done. So the directing leadership style is more directive than supportive. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 30

choices.text: Provide a more directive than supportive leadership.
Provide a more supportive than directive leadership.
Provide high levels of both directive and supportive leadership.
Provide low levels of both directive and supportive leadership.

text: In scrum of scrums daily meeting, the representative of each team should answer an extra question more than the questions asked in the daily scrum. What is this question?
explanation: The daily meeting is conducted similar to the daily standup in Scrum where the representative reports completed work, next set of work, any current impediments, and potential upcoming impediments that might block the other team(s). Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 111

choices.text: Is there any risks that may impact the overall product of the project?
Is there potential upcoming impediments that might block the other team?
Did your team establish a reliable velocity?
Are there any user stories still not assigned to team members?
